steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Michael sarcastically says Owen would carry a leaky pail on his head instead of a hat, just to have something to complain about. This reveals Michael's view of Owen's character — not anything about weather or hay.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Owen is worried that Michael is unaware of the coming rain.: Michael's line is about Owen's tendency to complain, not Owen's concern that Michael is unaware of the rain. ✗
  • Choice B — Owen will be able to help Michael with cutting the hay.: nothing in the exchange suggests cooperative intent from Owen. ✗
  • Choice C — Owen is a poor judge of the weather.: the pail-on-head image is about inventing reasons to complain, not about weather accuracy. ✗
  • Choice D — Owen will find fault with any situation.: the sarcasm directly implies Owen would manufacture a complaint even where none exists — perpetually fault-finding. ✓
That is the correct answer.
